revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ashitaka-jinja]][[ja:足高神社]] {{Expand Japanese|足高神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ashitaka Shrine | native_name = 足高神社 | image = Ashitaka Jinja 07.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ashitaka Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.577497|133.764541}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ashitaka Shrine'''|足高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11387310|ltq=Q11387310|qid=Q11387310|1=List of Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Momoiyamano Shrine|lt=Momoiyamano Shrine|qid=Q135041216|1=Momoiyamano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041218|ltq=Q135041218|qid=Q135041218|1=Sukafuno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q11602627|ltq=Q11602627|qid=Q11602627|1=Sasaoki}} * {{ill|Bitchū Province|lt=Bitchū Province|cs|Provincie Biččú|de|Provinz Bitchū|en|Bitchū Province|es|Provincia de Bitchū|fa|ولایت بیچو|fi|Bitchūn provinssi|fr|Province de Bitchū|gl|Provincia de Bitchū|id|Provinsi Bitchū|it|Bitchū|ja|備中国|ko|빗추국|lt|Bičiū žemė|nl|Bitchu (provincie)|pt|Província de Bitchū|ru|Биттю (провинция)|simple|Bitchū Province|sk|Biččú|sr|Провинција Бичу|tr|Bitchū (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Біттю|zh_min_nan|Bittyû-kok|zh|備中國|qid=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}} * {{ill|Q11597661|ltq=Q11597661|qid=Q11597661|1=Kuboya district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.577497|133.764541}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Ashitaka Jinja 07.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183086 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 813146998435018940073 == postal code (P281) == * 710-0834https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16671/ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3260005003812 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00650709 == Commons category (P373) == * Ashitaka-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県倉敷市笹沖1033', 'language': 'ja'}https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/16671/ {{commons category|Category:Ashitaka-jinja}} [[JA:足高神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11637069}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Ashitaka Shrine |Image=[[File:Ashitaka Jinja 07.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location=Okayama Prefecture Kurashiki City Sasaoki 1033 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities=(Primary deity) Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto
(Associated deities) Iwanaga-hime-no-Mikoto · Konohana-Sakuya-hime-no-Mikoto |Founded=(Traditionally) reign of Emperor Sujin |Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (small shrine)
Former prefectural shrine |Annual festival=Second Saturday of October |Main hall=Nagare-zukuri (Shinto architectural style) |Map= {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Ashitaka Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ashitaka Shrine''' (Ashitaka-jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Sasaoki, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. === Overview === It is a small shrine listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, and under the modern shrine ranking system its former status was that of a prefectural shrine. The enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto, with Iwanaga-hime-no-Mikoto and Konohana-Sakuya-hime-no-Mikoto as associated deities; there is also a theory that the deity is Ashinadaka-no-Kami. Its divine rank is Shō Shii-jō (Senior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade). It is also known by the alternate name “Honage no Miya” (Shrine of Lowered Sails). Until the late Edo period, it was written as “Ashitaka Shrine” using different characters. The shrine is situated on the summit of Mount Ashitaka, at an elevation of 67 meters, in the southern part of central Kurashiki City. Although it is now connected to the mainland, it was formerly an island floating in what was known as the Kibi no Anami, separated from Kojima (present-day Kojima Peninsula) in the Seto Inland Sea, and was called “Kotake-jima” or “Sasa-jima.” It was also said to have been known by other names such as Tojima, Fujito-jima, Kibi no Kojima, and Okutsu-jima. Mount Ashitaka became connected to the mainland through land reclamation carried out from the Azuchi–Momoyama period through the early Edo period. Throughout the Edo period, the shrine served as a place of prayer for the Kamogata Domain, a branch domain of the Okayama Domain, and the domain lord would visit the shrine upon each succession. Today, the area around the shrine on the mountaintop is maintained as Ashitaka Park, and in spring it becomes lively with cherry blossom viewers. === History === * According to shrine tradition, it was transferred to and enshrined at this location during the reign of Emperor Sujin. * In the Heian period, the name “Ashitaka Shrine, Kuboya District” is recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. * In the first year of Tenryaku (947), Emperor Murakami dispatched Fujiwara no Kanenari to conduct a ritual of offering sacred offerings and prayers. It is said that the shrine buildings were constructed in the third year of Tenryaku (949). * In the first year of Kanna (985), Jinyūzan Jingū-ji Henjō-in was established as the shrine temple (jingū-ji) of Ashitaka Shrine. * In the first year of Kangen (1243), a calligraphic plaque bestowed by imperial decree was granted to Ashitaka Hachiman (Hachiman Daibosatsu). === Shrine of Lowered Sails === When it was still an island known as “Kotake-jima” or “Sasa-jima,” the surrounding waters were said to have strong tidal currents and swirling eddies. All ships passing through would lower their sails and pray to the deity of Ashitaka for safe passage, which is how it came to be called the “Shrine of Lowered Sails.” The present place name “Sasaoki,” where the shrine is located, is derived from the fact that it was once “offshore of Sasa-jima.” === References === * Shinichi Yakushiji (author), *Ancient Kibi Seen Through Rituals*, Kibito Publishing, 1999 * *Ashitaka Shrine Shrine History*, Ashitaka Shrine, March 1995. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/14075193 (accessed 2025-06-23) * On-site information boards === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}} * Sasaoki {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ashitaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ashitaka Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]]
